summary: Knowledge website redesign is in final review, China Going-Global report is the major Q3 priority, and Intelligent China program launch is urgent this week.
summaryDetail: The knowledge website redesign is the most active project right now: the team is merging Joker's improved menu structure with Joseph's editorial-style homepage to show more articles and indices (BCI, BSI), with feedback due this week. The China Going-Global report (working title 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global') with IE is the flagship Q3 initiative, targeting a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore; the team is translating the Chinese report and drafting op-eds from deans. Urgent this week: the Intelligent China program webpage must go live to start SCM campaign planning, and the Asia Star campaign is launching with two routes (AI vs traditional). SEO prioritization is focusing on Asia Star and Intelligent China, with Google Analytics conversion tracking setup needed. Social media agency selection is resolved (Media Minds), and the Shun Tian Shu press release approach is finalized. The CKGSB-EuroCham event blurb and PO are due this week, and the Professor Mei Jianping report news release was distributed on July 8.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed two homepage design proposals for the knowledge website, preferring Joker's version with improved menu categorization but noting it's too similar to current site. They discussed combining elements from Joseph's more editorial-style design to show more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO and Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses improving search visibility for CKGSB programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China, and reviewing Google Analytics conversion tracking setup.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on website menu, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report
meetings.summary: Team discussed joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from deans and case studies. Report to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and a new Intelligent China program launching in December. Need to start SCM campaign for the new program once the webpage is ready.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on the importance of Europe-China relations, geopolitical tensions affecting companies, and the role of governments versus companies in trade.

decisions.text: Keep knowledge website's current look and feel, but improve menu categorization.
Remove dedicated video section; videos will be integrated by topic.
Show more articles on homepage, inspired by editorial style.
Include indices (BCI, BSI) on knowledge homepage.
Asia Star and Intelligent China are top two program priorities; US programs (Stanford, Columbia, Berkeley) are secondary.
Will focus on outcomes and target audience research rather than traditional program listings due to lack of degree programs for foreigners.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating the 10 points or turning it into a 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to budget constraints and time sensitivity.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Leeway and op-eds from both schools.
decisions.meetingTitle: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report

decisions.text: Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report

decisions.text: Report to be ready by early September for Singapore events.
decisions.meetingTitle: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report

decisions.text: Asia Start campaign will run two parallel routes (AI and traditional) for the first month.
Intelligent China program targets foreigners, is in English, and focuses on module one first.
Use the editorial design direction for Knowledge revamp
Use Flow for design of China Going Global report instead of in-house design
